pattern: The operation count for a given k drops as k grows while k-squared grows, so the feasibility check is monotonic and the minimum k is found with binary search.
hints: For a fixed k, the number of subtractions needed for one element is a ceiling division - what closed form gives the total operations for the whole array?
As k increases, the total operation count only ever goes down while k squared only ever goes up - once the condition holds for some k, does it keep holding for every larger k?
Binary search on k: for each candidate compute sum(ceil(nums[i]/k)) and compare against k*k, growing the search bound geometrically first to make sure a feasible k is inside it.

edge_inputs.description: single element at the maximum value
one huge value mixed with a tiny one
several elements all at the maximum value

stress.description: large array with a broad spread of values
